title: raphanus sativus subf. sylvestris
intro: Raphanus sativus subf. sylvestris, also known as wild radish, is a subspecies of the radish plant that grows in the wild. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of wild radish include reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Wild radish can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, or used raw in salads, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
